ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for genmaicha (roasted rice green tea). Closest defensible match is green tea beverage (FOODON_03301103); genmaicha is a subtype blending green tea with roasted brown rice.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated genmaicha code. A00LC (green tea, ready-to-drink) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Genmaicha is a blended green tea with roasted rice; the RTD no-sugar formulation aligns with this category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for genmaicha or roasted rice tea as a distinct clinical food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard packaged bottle of roasted rice tea (genmaicha) is approximately 350 ml, equivalent to 350 g, providing approximately 49 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Roasted Rice Tea (No Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09020063 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source without modification.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — unsweetened green tea and low-sugar tea beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged genmaicha (roasted rice green tea, no sugar). GI estimated at approximately 30 by analogy with unsweetened green tea and low-sugar tea beverages documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr, 2008; Foster-Powell et al., 2002). The low carbohydrate content (3.41 g/100 ml, predominantly naturally occurring sugars from roasted rice and tea) and absence of added sugar support a low GI classification. Glycemic load per 350 ml serving = (30 × 3.41 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 3.6; rounded to 3.6 per serving. Note: GL is clinically negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (7 mg/100 ml) and no added sugar; green tea polyphenols may support vascular health.
hypertension.guidance: Roasted rice tea (genmaicha) is appropriate for individuals with hypertension given its negligible sodium content (7 mg/100 ml); however, caffeine from the green tea component may cause transient blood pressure elevation in sensitive individuals, so consumption should be limited to 2–3 cups per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; low GI (~30); naturally occurring sugars at 2.89 g/100 ml pose negligible glycaemic risk.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This no-added-sugar packaged genmaicha has a low estimated GI (~30) and only 3.41 g carbohydrate per 100 ml (all naturally occurring), making it a suitable beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; green tea catechins (EGCG) may additionally support insulin sensitivity per published clinical evidence.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, negligible sodium, and green tea polyphenols with established cardioprotective properties.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted rice tea (genmaicha) contains no fat, no saturated fat, and negligible sodium, and the green tea polyphenols (catechins, EGCG) have demonstrated cardioprotective effects in meta-analyses; it is a suitable daily beverage for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease, provided caffeine intake is monitored.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins (EGCG) are established anti-inflammatory compounds; beverage is free of pro-inflammatory components.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Genmaicha contains green tea polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties consistent with EULAR dietary guidance; individuals on methotrexate or biologics should consult their pharmacist regarding caffeine interactions before regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted rice tea (genmaicha, no sugar, packaged) is a ready-to-drink Japanese beverage made from a blend of green tea leaves and roasted brown rice (genmai), commercially packaged without any added sugar. Genmaicha has a distinctive nutty, toasty flavour derived from the roasted rice, balanced by the mild grassy notes of green tea, resulting in a smooth, light-bodied drink with a warm golden-brown colour. It is consumed hot or cold and is widely available as a packaged RTD beverage across Asia. Nutritionally, it is very low in calories (14 kcal/100 ml), contains negligible protein (0.05 g/100 ml), zero fat, and a modest 3.41 g carbohydrate per 100 ml from naturally occurring sugars in the tea and rice — no sugar is added. Sodium is negligible at 7 mg/100 ml. The beverage is rich in green tea polyphenols including catechins and EGCG, which have documented anti-inflammatory, cardioprotective, and insulin-sensitising properties in peer-reviewed literature. Its low GI (~30, estimated by analogy with unsweetened green tea), absence of added sugar, and negligible sodium make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. A typical serving is one 350 ml bottle providing approximately 49 kcal.
common_user_queries: Is roasted rice tea low in sugar?
Can I drink genmaicha if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in a bottle of genmaicha?
Does roasted rice tea have caffeine?
What is the GI of genmaicha?
Is genmaicha suitable for weight loss?
What is the difference between genmaicha and green tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020063, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Source match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for unsweetened green tea beverages, which is the closest approved GI source available; this reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the low carbohydrate content and clinical negligibility of the GL.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo source — this is a water-based tea beverage containing no fat by formulation (confirmed zero). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same source and by the nature of the product. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — the beverage is a liquid extract with no intact fibre (confirmed zero). calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero per approved source). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged RTD genmaicha product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D09020063 and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an exact genmaicha RTD match as of 2026. GI value of 30 is an analogous estimate based on unsweetened green tea and low-sugar tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for packaged genmaicha was located in approved sources. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been removed as genmaicha contains 3.41 g carbohydrate per 100 ml, which is not consistent with strict ketogenic dietary criteria for beverages. Glycemic load per 350 ml serving calculated as (30 × 3.41 × 3.5) / 100 = 3.58, rounded to 3.6.
